title: Domain type and validation
text: The system shall define a Domain type (integer) validated to the range 0–232 inclusive, returning ErrDomainOutOfRange for values outside that range.

title: GUID type
text: The system shall define a Guid type as a 16-byte fixed-size array, matching the DDS writer GUID encoding in relay::Message meta key dds.writer_guid as a 32-character lowercase hex string.

title: QoS types and defaults
text: The system shall define ReliabilityKind (BestEffort=0, Reliable=1), DurabilityKind (Volatile=0, TransientLocal=1), and QoS struct with default_qos() and reliable_qos() factory functions.

title: Sample type and message conversion
text: The system shall define a Sample struct and implement lossless Sample::to_message() and from_message() per RELAY spec §15.7.2, including topic, payload, timestamp, seq, and writer_guid. The encoding must match the RELAY spec golden vector (topic=rt/chatter, seq=7, guid bytes 1–16, meta dds.writer_guid=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10).

title: IPublisher interface
text: The system shall define an IPublisher abstract interface with write(payload), write(ctx, payload), and close() methods, returning ErrPayloadTooLarge when QoS.max_sample_size is exceeded and ErrTimeout when the context has expired.

title: ISubscriber interface
text: The system shall define an ISubscriber abstract interface with channel(), try_read(), unsubscribe(), and close() methods per RELAY spec §8.2.

title: IParticipant interface
text: The system shall define an IParticipant abstract interface with new_publisher(), new_subscriber(), domain(), and close() methods per RELAY spec §8.2.

title: DDS error sentinels
text: The system shall define a dds error category and the DDS-specific error codes ErrTopicEmpty, ErrQoSMismatch, ErrDeadlineMissed, ErrSampleRejected, ErrResourceLimits, ErrLoanBuffer, ErrDomainOutOfRange, each mapping to the closest relay sentinel per RELAY spec §5.3.

title: adapt() relay::INode bridge
text: The system shall provide adapt() to wrap an IParticipant as a relay::INode, forwarding send/subscribe/close and translating relay::Message ↔ dds::Sample per §15.7.2.

title: SampleFilter and with_filter subscriber option
text: The system shall provide a SampleFilter predicate type and a with_filter(pred) subscriber option that silently drops samples not matching the predicate before they reach the subscriber channel.

title: with_deadline_missed subscriber option
text: The system shall provide a with_deadline_missed(callback) subscriber option that invokes the callback when QoS.deadline elapses without a sample being received on the subscriber.

title: WaitSet — multiplex subscriber channels
text: The system shall provide a WaitSet class that multiplexes multiple subscriber channels onto a single wait_any() call, returning the first available sample and its source index. Equivalent to go-DDS WaitSet.

title: ILoaningPublisher — zero-copy loan-based write
text: The system shall provide an ILoaningPublisher interface extending IPublisher with loan_buffer(), write_loaned(), and return_loan() for zero-copy sample publishing equivalent to go-DDS LoaningPublisher (RELAY spec §8.3).

title: Mock participant factory
text: The system shall provide dds::mock::create(domain) returning an IMockParticipant that validates the domain and returns ErrDomainOutOfRange for out-of-range values.

title: Mock participant lifecycle
text: The mock participant's close() shall be idempotent; subsequent new_publisher() and new_subscriber() calls after close shall return ErrClosed.

title: Mock publish and in-process routing
text: Publishing a sample via IPublisher::write() shall deliver it to all matching subscribers in the same process via the global Broker, including monotonically increasing sequence numbers.

title: Mock subscriber channel and back-pressure
text: Each subscriber shall receive an independent channel with configurable depth and back-pressure policy (DropNewest, DropOldest, Block).

title: Mock unsubscribe semantics
text: After Unsubscribe() the channel is closed, further sends are silently dropped, and calling Unsubscribe() again is a no-op per RELAY spec §6.4.

title: Write metrics tracking
text: The mock participant shall track write_count and bytes_written incremented atomically on each successful IPublisher::write() call.

title: Delivery metrics tracking
text: The mock participant shall track deliver_count, drop_count, and bytes_delivered reflecting actual broker delivery outcomes per publish cycle.

title: IMetricsProvider interface
text: The mock participant shall implement relay::IMetricsProvider, exposing an atomic snapshot of all counters via metrics() with no side effects.

title: IHealthProvider — healthy status
text: The mock participant shall implement relay::IHealthProvider, returning HealthStatus::OK while active.

title: IHealthProvider — degraded on close
text: After close() the mock participant's health() shall return HealthStatus::Down.

title: Domain boundary enforcement
text: The system shall reject any domain identifier outside 0–232 inclusive before any object is created, preventing access to out-of-range DDS domains.

title: Payload size enforcement
text: When QoS.max_sample_size > 0 the system shall reject payloads exceeding that size with ErrPayloadTooLarge, preventing heap exhaustion from oversized samples.

title: Topic name validation
text: The system shall reject empty topic names with ErrTopicEmpty at the IParticipant level, preventing creation of publishers or subscribers on unnamed topics.

title: Write-after-close rejection
text: IPublisher::write() calls on a closed publisher or participant shall return ErrClosed without publishing any data.

title: Concurrent state access safety
text: All shared mutable state (Broker subscriptions, last-sample cache, participant counters) shall be protected by appropriate synchronisation primitives, ensuring data-race freedom under concurrent access.

title: Channel depth cap
text: The system shall define kMaxChanDepth = 4096 as the maximum allowed channel depth, preventing unbounded memory allocation from misconfigured subscribers.

title: IPublisher idempotent close
text: IPublisher::close() shall be idempotent: calling it multiple times shall not produce errors or undefined behaviour.

title: ISubscriber idempotent unsubscribe
text: ISubscriber::unsubscribe() shall be idempotent: calling it multiple times or after close() shall be a no-op.

title: IParticipant idempotent close
text: IParticipant::close() shall be idempotent: repeated calls shall return success without side effects.

title: Post-close participant operations
text: After IParticipant::close(), new_publisher() and new_subscriber() shall return ErrClosed with a null pointer.

title: Subscriber destructor auto-unsubscribes
text: The ISubscriber destructor shall automatically call unsubscribe(), ensuring the channel is closed and broker routing is cleaned up even if the caller does not explicitly unsubscribe.

title: Chan<T> bounded FIFO semantics
text: Chan<T> shall be a bounded FIFO channel with a configurable capacity; elements are delivered in the order inserted. The channel shall be non-copyable.

title: Chan<T> non-blocking try_send / try_recv
text: try_send() and try_recv() shall return immediately without blocking, returning Full/Closed or nullopt as appropriate.

title: Chan<T> closed-channel semantics
text: recv() on a closed and empty channel shall return nullopt immediately; send() on a closed channel shall return false immediately.

title: Chan<T> back-pressure policies
text: The Broker shall support three back-pressure policies: DropNewest (try_send, drop incoming when full), DropOldest (evict head, insert new), and Block (send, block sender until space available).

title: CLI: version subcommand
text: The cpp-dds CLI version subcommand shall print the RELAY spec version (1.7), the DDS protocol identifier (2), and the library semantic version.

title: CLI: conform subcommand
text: The cpp-dds CLI conform subcommand shall invoke the RELAY conform gate and exit with code 0 on success, non-zero on failure.

title: CLI: convert subcommand
text: The cpp-dds CLI convert subcommand shall translate relay::Message JSON between protocol representations, printing the result to stdout.

title: Thread-safe shared state
text: All shared mutable state shall be protected by std::mutex, std::shared_mutex, or std::atomic; no data races shall be detectable under ASan/TSan analysis.

title: Error-code return discipline
text: All public API functions shall return std::error_code for failure signalling; no exceptions shall be thrown across public API boundaries.

title: Bounded execution time
text: All blocking API operations shall accept a relay::Context with timeout so callers can bound execution time and detect liveness failures.

title: Requirements traceability
text: Every source file shall carry fusa:req annotations linking implementation units to requirement identifiers; every test file shall carry fusa:test annotations linking test cases to the requirements they verify. The CI fusa-asil-b job shall validate that all requirements are both implemented and tested.

title: RELAY spec golden vector conformance
text: Sample::to_message() and from_message() must produce output that exactly matches the RELAY spec §15.7.2 golden vector: topic=rt/chatter, payload=[104,101,108,108,111,32,100,100,115], seq=7, writer_guid bytes [1..16], meta dds.writer_guid=0102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f10.
